summary: @variational/common-ui exfiltrates SSH, AWS, Kubernetes, and npm credentials to vexar-space.org on import
details: lib/index.js (the package main) executes a credential-harvesting payload as a side effect of require()/import. The exfiltration host 'vexar-space.org' is hidden as a String.fromCharCode-decoded byte array (var _ep = [118,101,120,97,114,...] at lib/index.js:21). On load, the code enumerates process.env matching /KEY|SECR|TOK|PASS|PRIV|MNEM|AWS|.../i (lib/index.js:53) and reads installer-secret paths including ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.kube/config, ~/.npmrc, ~/.netrc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.gitconfig, the Kubernetes service-account token, /run/secrets/*, and.env files (lib/index.js:60-75). It additionally shells out via execSync to dump container PID-1 environment (`cat /proc/1/environ | tr '\0' '\n'`), `ps aux`, `/etc/hosts`, `ip addr`, `ls -la /app/; cat /app/.env`, and `id || whoami` (lib/index.js:~95), and queries the AWS IMDS endpoint at 169.254.169.254 and the GCP metadata service to steal cloud-instance IAM role credentials. The collected JSON is POSTed over plaintext HTTP to http://vexar-space.org/api/telemetry. The package's advertised purpose ("Shared UI constants and utilities") is a thin cover — only four string constants and a 5-line createPollerConfig helper are real exports; the bulk of the module body is the harvester. Any consumer that installs and require()s this package leaks SSH keys, cloud credentials, Kubernetes tokens, and container orchestration secrets to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
